rulings.Case Title: MARIA CHAVEZ VS. F&M OBERTI INC et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2009, line 1, DEFENDANT F&M OBERTI INC, and, MARCELLA OBERTI MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Continued to September 23, 2009 pursuant to CCP section 437c(h) to allow Plaintiff's expert to conduct a site inspection. Any disputes as to the timelines of the additional discovery requests are to be raised in the Discovery Department. Plaintiff's supplemental opposition is due on September 11, 2009. Defendant's Supplemental reply is due on September 17, 2009. Supplemental briefs are not to exceed ten pages. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: DIAMOND HEIGHTS VILLAGE ASSOCIATION, INC. VS. MARK WILLIAMS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2009, line 11, DEFENDANT FINANCIAL FREEDOM SENIOR FUNDING CORPORATION, Motion For Attorneys Fees And Costs. Motion denied. Review of Judge Davis's decision and the supplemental Briefs does not alter the court's tentative decision. There is no privity between plaintiff and defendant and the case concerned priority. Clar vs. Cacciola (1987) 193 Cal.App.3d 1032 is the controlling authority. No basis for recovery under section 1367. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: THOMAS F. LUM VS. WILLIAM A. ORELLANA et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2009, line 12, DEFENDANT WILLIAM ORELLANA, JOSE ORTIZ, and, MARCO GONZALEZ MOTION TO TAX COSTS. Motion denied. Plaintiff made a valid section 998 offer to settle with defendant Gonzalez that he did not accept. The judgment received by plaintiff was more favorable than the offer. Defendant Orellana is only liabile for $15,000.00 and the remaining two defendants are jointly and severally liable for $565,955.00. Formuzi's fee reduced to $4,225.00 and Slosar fee reduced to $3,500.00. Request for trial courior costs of $709.80, and attorney transportation expenses of $235.50 withdrawn. The challenged $840.00 is allowable court reporter per diem fees. The court adopts plaintiff's proposed order. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: F. ALIOTO COMPANY, INC, A CALIFORNIA VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO, A MUNICIPAL et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2009, line 8, DEFENDANT CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Demurrer sustained with ten days leave to amend as to the fourth and fifth causes of action. Plaintiff to plead compliance with the Government Claims Act, excuse, or seek only non-monetary equitable relief. =(302/CWW)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT S. VAILE VS. DELOITTE & TOUCHE, LLP
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Robert S. Vaile'S Order To Show Cause Re Preliminary Injunction
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 27, 2009, line 9, Plaintiff Robert S. Vaile's Order to Show Cause regarding preliminary injunction. Hearing required for plaintiff to provide to the court copies of the proofs of service of the amended complaint. Upon proper proofs of service, motion granted. Plaintiff likely to prevail due to lack of a properly domesticated order. See Code of Civil Procedure section 1710.15 et seq. Plaintiff will suffer irreparable harm without the injunction due to lack of funds to meet plaintiff's nondiscretionary obligations. Plaintiff to post a bond in the amount of $10,000 on or before September 4, 2009. (CCP section 529.) =(302/CWW)
